mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier discounting and liquidity support to equities, so a 3-month downtrend in policy rates predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when rates are already near zero and further cuts do not change risk premia.
failure_condition: If triggered by a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, the average 20-day SPX return is not positive and Rank IC stays below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window, especially when FEDFUNDS is near the effective lower bound.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE indicates accelerating labor-market deterioration, reducing earnings expectations and risk appetite, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when unemployment is rising faster than recent payroll growth can offset it.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if, after UNRATE rises by 0.3pp in 3 months, SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases or the conditional mean return is not negative at conventional significance.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS and RSAFS both improve, the economy is simultaneously adding jobs and sustaining consumer demand, which supports revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if sales strengthen without labor confirmation or if payroll gains are purely backward-looking revisions.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS does not outperform either series alone, or if the 60-day forward SPX return is not positive when both series rise over the prior 2 months.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO trend signals stronger industrial output and capacity utilization, which improves cyclical earnings expectations and tends to lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should vanish when production gains are driven by temporary inventory restocking rather than final demand.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not above baseline after two straight months of INDPRO gains, or if the conditional hit rate is not better than 50% over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation coupled with low policy rates implies negative real policy rates, which supports equity valuations over the next 20 trading days by keeping financing conditions loose. This signal fails when inflation rises but FEDFUNDS rises immediately enough to preserve positive real rates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if periods with inflation exceeding FEDFUNDS by 2pp do not show higher 20-day SPX returns than matched controls, or if the relationship flips sign when real rates are positive.

mechanism_narrative: A weak housing starts reading indicates a cooling interest-sensitive demand segment that often precedes slower broad activity and lower equity returns over the next 60 trading days. The decline should be most informative when it persists rather than appearing as a one-month noise shock.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below its 6-month average is not followed by below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears after controlling for general market trend.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE identifies a genuine labor-market contraction, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings and consumption expectations weaken. Either series alone is insufficient because the joint move distinguishes true deterioration from measurement noise.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines while UNRATE rises within the same 2-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up condition does not produce negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not stronger than either series used alone.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales alongside rising industrial production indicates synchronized demand and production momentum, supporting earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when one component improves but the other remains flat, because that implies incomplete cyclical confirmation.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous gains in RSAFS and INDPRO do not lead to higher 60-day SPX returns than either proxy alone, or if the conditional mean return is not positive across triggered episodes.
